title: Ensure that 'Number of days before users are asked to re- confirm their authentication information' is not set to '0'

input_row.title: Ensure that 'Number of days before users are asked to re- confirm their authentication information' is not set to '0'
input_row.description: Ensure that the number of days before users are asked to re-confirm their authentication information is not set to 0.
input_row.rationale: This setting is necessary if 'Require users to register when signing in' is enabled. If authentication re-confirmation is disabled, registered users will never be prompted to re- confirm their existing authentication information. If the authentication information for a user changes, such as a phone number or email, then the password reset information for that user reverts to the previously registered authentication information.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID. 3. Under Manage, select Users. 4. Select Password reset. 5. Under Manage, select Registration. 6. Ensure that Number of days before users are asked to re-confirm their authentication information is not set to 0.
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu. 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID. 3. Under Manage, select Users. 4. Select Password reset. 5. Under Manage, select Registration. 6. Set the Number of days before users are asked to re-confirm their authentication information to your organization-defined frequency. 7. Click Save.
input_row.impact: Users will be prompted to re-confirm their authentication information after the number of days specified.
input_row.default_value: By default, the Number of days before users are asked to re-confirm their authentication information is set to "180 days".

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**MANUAL STEPS:**
1. **Access the Azure Portal:**
   - Open your web browser and go to the Azure portal (https://portal.azure.com).
  
2. **Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID:**
   - From the Azure Home screen, select the Portal Menu (three horizontal lines/icon or ‘hamburger menu’) on the left side.
   - Choose “Microsoft Entra ID” from the list of available services.

3. **Select Users:**
   - In the Microsoft Entra ID dashboard, find and click on “Users” under the Manage section.

4. **Access Password Reset Settings:**
   - Once in the Users section, select the “Password reset” option.

5. **Manage Registration Settings:**
   - Under the Password Reset menu, select the “Registration” option to access settings related to user authentication information.

6. **Verify the Days Before Re-confirmation:**
   - Look for the setting labelled “Number of days before users are asked to re-confirm their authentication information.”
   - Check the configured value to ensure it is not set to "0".

7. **Expected Secure Configuration:**
   - The configuration should specify a numerical value greater than 0, indicating the number of days users are required to re-confirm authentication (for example, "180 days" is the default setting).

8. **Confirm Compliance:**
   - If the configuration is greater than 0, document this as compliant. If it is set to 0, recommend that the setting be adjusted according to organizational policy to mitigate potential security risks associated with unchanged authentication information. 

By following these steps, you can manually verify compliance with the specified control regarding user authentication information re-confirmation settings.
